part.text: The target file has the same vulnerable pattern in `QuantizeAndDequantizeV3Op::Compute`: it reads `input.dim_size(axis_)` before checking that `axis_` is within `input.dims()`. I’m adding the same guard immediately after reading `input`, matching the newer fix.

part.text:
```yaml
backport_result:
  status: success
  patch: |
    diff --git a/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_and_dequantize_op.cc b/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_and_dequantize_op.cc
    index 8f71d09c08..a66ba66a98 100644
    --- a/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_and_dequantize_op.cc
    +++ b/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_and_dequantize_op.cc
    @@ -13,6 +13,7 @@ See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
     limitations under the License.
     ==============================================================================*/
     
    +#include "tensorflow/core/framework/op_requires.h"
     #define EIGEN_USE_THREADS
     
     #if (defined(GOOGLE_CUDA) && GOOGLE_CUDA) || \
    @@ -151,6 +152,10 @@ class QuantizeAndDequantizeV3Op : public OpKernel {
     
       void Compute(OpKernelContext* ctx) override {
         const Tensor& input = ctx->input(0);
    +    OP_REQUIRES(ctx, axis_ < input.dims(),
    +                errors::InvalidArgument(
    +                    "Axis requested is larger than input dimensions. Axis: ",
    +                    axis_, " Input Dimensions: ", input.dims()));
         const int depth = (axis_ == -1) ? 1 : input.dim_size(axis_);
         Tensor* output = nullptr;
         OP_REQUIRES_OK(ctx, ctx->allocate_output(0, input.shape(), &output));
  notes: >
    Added the missing axis bounds check to QuantizeAndDequantizeV3Op before input.dim_size(axis_) is evaluated.
```
